questions.q: Which animal is famous for playing dead when threatened, even sticking its tongue out to complete the act?
What color were carrots originally before farmers bred them to be orange?
Which planet in our solar system rotates on its side, unlike all the others?
Which famous Renaissance inventor wrote his notes backwards in his journals?
Which TV personality famously announced the wrong winner at the 2015 Miss Universe pageant?
What is the smallest country in the world, located entirely within another city?
Which continent has no native penguin populations?
Which actor not only starred in films but also performed as the lead singer in a band called Dead Man's Bones?
What iconic Paris landmark was originally designed to be a temporary structure for the 1889 World's Fair?
Which US President delivered the Gettysburg Address, beginning with 'Four score and seven years ago'?
What is the world record for the most spoons balanced on a person's face at one time?
What video game character has been jumping over barrels and saving princesses since the 1980s?
Which actress is known for having the middle name 'Love' and starred in a paranormal TV series?
What was the primary purpose of pocket watches before wristwatches became popular?
Which fruit gets its name from the Arabic word for 'finger' because of its finger-like shape?
In which US state would you find college basketball teams like the Tar Heels and the Blue Devils?
In the TV show 'Friends', what was the name of Ross's pet monkey?
Which month of the year has the fewest days?
In Greek mythology, who is the ruler of the underworld?
What city is commonly known as 'The Big Apple'?
Which element on the periodic table smells like rotten eggs?
What is the world's most commonly stolen food item?
In the 'Harry Potter' series, how many points does catching the Golden Snitch award to a Quidditch team?
Which Australian landmark resembles giant sails and sits in a famous harbour?
What is the national animal of Scotland?
Which pop star had a tulip flower species named after her?
What did early hockey players use as pucks before rubber was invented?
In 2004, which pop singer had a notorious lip-sync fail on 'Saturday Night Live'?
Which US President became famously stuck in a bathtub?
What fermented ingredient is a key component of miso soup?
Which animal has the largest brain of any creature on Earth?
In the movie 'The Nutty Professor', which actor played an entire family dinner scene by himself?
Which sport is considered the fastest racquet sport in the world?
What nickname for British police officers comes from the Metropolitan Police founder Sir Robert Peel?
What was ketchup originally marketed as in the 1830s?
Which TV show featured a famous scene with a pizza being thrown onto a rooftop?
What animal has fingerprints nearly identical to human fingerprints?
Which month of the year always has exactly 30 days?
What is the name of the mustachioed video game plumber created by Nintendo?
Which professional basketball player famously wore a size 22 shoe, making him one of the few athletes to have custom footwear designed specifically for his unusual foot size?
In 1983, a Japanese game show contestant won a Nissan Skyline by correctly answering a trivia question about what unexpected subject matter?
choices: The scientific name of a common household insect
The molecular composition of car paint
The exact weight of a specific car model
The number of grains of rice in a cup

questions.q: Which European monarch was so obsessed with collecting that he amassed over 3,000 items of a single category, eventually requiring an entire wing of his palace?
choices: King Frederick II of Prussia - snuff boxes
King Charles VI of France - tapestries
King George III of England - buttons
King Louis XVI of France - pocket watches

questions.q: A competitive eating champion from California holds the record for consuming the most of which food item in ten minutes, downing 76 of them in 2021?
Which heavy metal band's bassist was once a professional soccer player before joining the music industry in the 1980s?
In the 1960s, a British artist named John Latham famously mailed pages of what to famous museums as part of a conceptual art protest?
Which Soviet cosmonaut accidentally recorded the first ever music played in space, though it was completely unintended and caused some confusion?
A wealthy English eccentric in the 1800s built an entire underground network beneath his estate to avoid seeing servants—what was his primary phobia?
Which fashion designer once hired a man whose sole job was to follow him around and collect his discarded cigarette butts throughout the day?
In 2019, a Japanese collector set a world record for owning the largest personal collection of what peculiar item—over 5,000 of them?
choices: Toy figurines of a single character

questions.q: A Canadian stuntman once survived going over Niagara Falls encased in what unconventional vessel in 1995?
Which British comedian famously donated his brain to science specifically so researchers could study why he was funny?
A software engineer from Silicon Valley once traded a red paperclip through 14 successive internet trades until he acquired what final item?
In the 1970s, which American musician wore an enormous codpiece on stage that weighed approximately 40 pounds?
A professional poker player from Las Vegas once won a tournament while blindfolded, relying entirely on which non-visual sense?
choices: A hidden earpiece communicating chip positions
His photographic memory of previous hands

questions.q: Which Academy Award-winning actor studied to become a priest before abandoning his religious studies to pursue drama in the 1970s?

In 2008, an Italian chef prepared a meal while performing a handstand, setting a Guinness World Record for the longest duration of doing so while cooking—how many minutes did he maintain this position?
A wealthy eccentric from Pennsylvania once hired a film crew to document himself doing mundane activities for 24 hours straight—what year did this experimental video first circulate?
Which NHL player famously kept a live animal in his locker room during the 1990s as a good luck charm, much to his teammates' bewilderment?
A theoretical physicist became famous in the 1980s for proposing that time could be experienced backwards if one consumed a specific combination of substances—who was this controversial figure?
In 1978, Steve Buscemi worked as a firefighter in which New York City borough before pursuing acting full-time?
Which actor famously refused to use a stunt double for the bathroom fight scene in 'They Live', resulting in a 5-minute brawl that exhausted both performers?
What is the name of the marsupial that actor Zoe Kazan owns, which she has documented extensively on social media?
In the 1987 film 'Mannequin', which department store was featured as the primary filming location?
Which obscure European record label released the first pressing of Joy Division's 'Unknown Pleasures' in 1979?
In what year did German artist Kurt Schwitters begin his famous Merzbau installation in his Hannover home?
Which British television personality holds the Guinness World Record for the longest continuous time spent standing on a treadmill?
What food item was humorously classified by the U.S. Congress in a 1981 memo as a vegetable for school lunch purposes?
In the early 1900s, which magician's act famously involved making a live elephant disappear on stage in front of thousands?
Which professional wrestler famously wore a mask throughout his entire career and never revealed his face to the public until his retirement?
In what year did the inventor of the shopping cart patent his wheeled basket device?
Which silent film actor performed all his own stunts, including jumping between buildings, and died from injuries sustained during filming?
What was the name of the inflatable man-shaped balloon character that appeared in British children's television in the 1970s?
In 1985, which music festival featured a satellite link-up between stadiums in London and Philadelphia to raise awareness for famine relief?
Which avant-garde artist created a piece titled 'Fountain' by submitting a porcelain urinal to an art exhibition in 1917?
In medieval times, which practice of bathing became so rare that nobility often wore perfume instead to mask body odor?
Which game show host famously inserted his own commentary into card reveals, creating a distinctive comedic style in the 1970s?
In 1962, which soft drink company attempted to compete with Coca-Cola by introducing a lemon-lime flavored beverage that eventually became the third-best-selling soda?
Which Japanese game show from the 1980s became infamous in Western countries for its extreme and often dangerous physical challenges?
In 1995, which talk show host famously gave away cars to every member of her audience, costing approximately $8 million for that single episode?
Which European city's main railway station has a statue of a man with his pants down, mooning train passengers as they arrive?
In competitive eating, what is the record number of hot dogs and buns consumed in 10 minutes, famously achieved at Nathan's Famous Fourth of July contest?
Which Nobel Prize-winning physicist also invented a type of refrigerator and held over 40 patents, despite having no formal engineering training?
In the Harry Potter series, what is the name of the three-headed dog that guards the Philosopher's Stone in the first book?
What was the name of the first person to be officially diagnosed with a fear of the number 13, a condition documented in psychiatric literature?
Which chess grandmaster famously played 56 opponents simultaneously while blindfolded in 1960, winning 50 games and drawing 6?
In 2011, which fast-food chain accidentally served a customer a chicken nugget shaped exactly like his home state, which he donated to a museum?
What is the technical term for the fear of long words, which is particularly ironic because the word for it is extremely long?
Which Antarctic research station is located exactly at the South Pole and must be rebuilt every few years because it sinks into the ice?
In medieval times, what was a 'tronage'—a tax that was often the subject of humorous complaints in historical documents?
choices: A tax on the weight of goods

questions.q: Which internet meme character, originally created as a placeholder image, became so popular it was featured in a museum exhibition?
What unusual claim did a Tennessee man make in 1954 that led him to attempt to patent the color beige?
choices: He discovered it naturally in nature
He invented a new method to produce it
It was his family's traditional hue
He argued it deserved protection as intellectual property

questions.q: Which sport's governing body once banned a competitor for life after he threw his shoe at an opponent in frustration?

What percentage of the human body is composed of water?

How much does an average cumulus cloud weigh?
